findings.reasoning: R-04 applies to advertisements stating a 'bonus' in connection with a bank/deposit account product (e.g., a checking or savings account opening bonus), requiring APY, time to obtain, minimum balance requirements, and timing disclosures. This page is a sweepstakes/giveaway promotion ('#TurboTaxAndRelax') offering cash prizes ($10,000 grand prize, etc.) awarded via random drawing to entrants who post a video on social media. This is not a deposit account bonus or any financial product bonus tied to opening/maintaining an account - it is a promotional sweepstakes prize with no account-opening component, no APY, no balance requirements, and no banking product being advertised at all. The rule's trigger (an institution stating a bonus in an advertisement for a bank product) is not met because there is no bank account product being promoted here - only a marketing sweepstakes for TurboTax tax-filing software users. The strongest counter-reading is that any cash award could be loosely termed a 'bonus,' but the rule's substantive disclosure items (APY, minimum balance to obtain/open, time to obtain) presuppose a deposit account context, which is entirely absent here. Therefore this rule does not apply to this page.

Rule R-04 concerns advertisements that state a bonus on a deposit account product, which would then require specific disclosures (APY, time requirements, minimum balances, and timing of bonus). This page promotes a sweepstakes/contest offering cash prizes (e.g., “one grand prize winner will receive TEN THOUSAND DOLLARS!”) and does not advertise any deposit account, banking product, or account-opening bonus. The only use of the word “bonus” is in a link to a “Bonus tax calculator,” which refers to employment/compensation bonuses, not a bank bonus. Therefore, the rule’s trigger condition is not met, and the rule is not applicable.

Rule R-04 concerns deposit account bonus advertising (e.g., APY, minimum balance, timing of bonus). This page promotes a social media sweepstakes with cash prizes and does not advertise any bank account or deposit bonus. There is no mention of an APY, account opening, minimum balance, or bonus tied to a deposit product. Footer links (e.g., Credit Karma Money, Bonus tax calculator) do not contain bonus claims on this page. Therefore, the rule’s trigger is not met.
